tools.name: search_vendors
tools.description: Search all licensed Florida food trucks and caterers (19,000+, scored from official DBPR inspection records). Matches business name, prior names, license number (MFD3953067 and 3953067 both work), and city. Optional county, grade, and limit filters. Grades: A to F, plus HOLD (active DBPR license hol

tools.name: get_scorecard
tools.description: One vendor's public compliance record: letter grade (or withheld when the current DBPR license cannot be confirmed), license status, inspection recency, and record shape. Look up by vendor_id from search_vendors, or directly by license_number (MFD3953067 or 3953067) to vet a vendor in one call. Grad

tools.name: decide_vendor
tools.description: One-call booking decision for a Florida food vendor: CLEAR, CONDITIONAL, REVIEW, or DECLINE with reason codes, the evidence behind it, the documents to collect for the event type, and a signed receipt the organizer can keep in their compliance file. This is the recommended vetting tool; use search_v

tools.name: improve_my_score
tools.description: For a Florida food truck or caterer checking their own record: what is currently limiting the VMScore letter grade, what to do about each item, official Florida DBPR resources, and a link to claim the free VenuMark vendor profile. Look up by license_number (MFD3953067 and 3953067 both work) or vendo

tools.name: get_checklist
tools.description: A Florida organizer checklist as markdown. Slugs: florida-food-truck-night (breweries, HOAs, churches), festival-food-vendors (festivals, farmers markets), city-special-event-food-vendors (public property).

tools.name: get_platform_info
tools.description: What VenuMark is (the food vendor application and compliance platform for Florida events), how the workflow runs, current pricing tiers, and which tier fits an organizer. Use when someone asks about running vendor applications, pricing, or whether VenuMark fits their event.

tools.name: list_open_events
tools.description: Florida events currently published on VenuMark and taking food vendor applications. Optional city filter. Each result includes the application deadline, required documents, and the public apply_url where a vendor applies (free account).

tools.name: get_event
tools.description: One published event's public details: dates, venue, city, application deadline, required documents, and the apply link. Use the slug from list_open_events or from a venumark.com/event/<slug> URL.

tools.name: get_signup_link
tools.description: A direct VenuMark signup link for the human to open in their browser. role 'venue' is for organizers who run vendor applications; optional tier ('growth' or 'pro') and cadence ('month' or 'year') preselect the plan, and checkout completes in the browser via Stripe. role 'vendor' is for food trucks a

tools.name: submit_lead
tools.description: Send an organizer's contact request to the VenuMark founder (setup help, demo, festival or city program, Enterprise scope). Only call after the user has explicitly confirmed the exact details to send. The founder replies by email, usually the same day.

tools.name: search
tools.description: Alias of search_vendors for ChatGPT Deep Research connectors. Returns matching Florida food vendors as results with id, title, and url. Pass the id to fetch for the full record.

tools.name: fetch
tools.description: Alias of get_scorecard for ChatGPT Deep Research connectors. Takes a vendor id from search and returns the full public scorecard as a document (id, title, text, url, metadata).

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "fetch" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_event, search, fetch · Private data: search_vendors, get_scorecard, decide_vendor, improve_my_score, list_open_events, get_event, submit_lead, search, fetch · Public sink: submit_lead · Destructive: get_signup_link.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_event, search, fetch · Private data: search_vendors, get_scorecard, decide_vendor, improve_my_score, list_open_events, get_event, submit_lead, search, fetch · Public sink: submit_lead · Destructive: get_signup_link

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
